name: Lamb Shanks in Red Mole Sauce
description: Lamb shanks braised in a red mole of chipotle, pasilla peppers, roasted tomatoes, and Mexican chocolate. A slow braise with a French-method sear and a Mexican sauce.
keywords: lamb shank recipe, halal lamb, mole sauce, chipotle, Mexican chocolate, braised lamb
recipeIngredient: 42 g (1.5 oz) Mexican chocolate, roughly chopped
Fine salt and black pepper (for lamb)

recipeInstructions.name: Rehydrate chipotles
recipeInstructions.text: Soak dried chipotle peppers in just-boiled water for 20 minutes. Drain, stem, and remove seeds for less heat.

recipeInstructions.name: Roast aromatics
recipeInstructions.text: Char plum tomatoes and garlic on a dry skillet. Blister pasilla peppers flat on the skillet for 15-20 seconds per side.

recipeInstructions.name: Blend mole base
recipeInstructions.text: Blend chipotles, pasilla peppers, roasted tomatoes, garlic, oregano, cinnamon, sesame seeds, black pepper, and tomato sauce until completely smooth.

recipeInstructions.name: Fry the mole
recipeInstructions.text: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Fry blended mole for 8 minutes, stirring frequently, until rust-brick in color. Season with salt.

recipeInstructions.name: Sear the lamb
recipeInstructions.text: Season lamb shanks with salt and pepper. Sear in 3 tbsp oil over high heat, in batches, 3-4 minutes per side until deep mahogany.

recipeInstructions.name: Braise
recipeInstructions.text: Return shanks to pot. Pour mole over and add 240 ml water. Bring to a simmer, cover, and braise at 160°C (325°F) for 2 to 2.5 hours, turning once.

recipeInstructions.name: Finish with chocolate
recipeInstructions.text: Remove pot from oven. Set over low heat. Add chopped Mexican chocolate and stir until melted. Do not boil after this point.

recipeInstructions.name: Rest and serve
recipeInstructions.text: Return shanks to sauce, spoon over mole, and rest 5 minutes before serving with rice or corn tortillas.
